title: How to set date to a *specific date* based on status
excerpt:
Is it possible to create an automation, or some sort of formula wizardry so when I change a Status the date is set to a specific date. 
The use case is effectively trying to use Monday.com for an agile approach (which I …

posts.author: Ashterix (Ash Bladon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it possible to create an automation, or some sort of formula wizardry so when I change a Status the date is set to a specific date.

The use case is effectively trying to use Monday.com for an agile approach (which I don’t think it’s really built for TBH). I’m mapping the Status to ‘Backlog’, ‘Phase 1’, ‘Phase 2’, ‘Phase 3’ etc, and I’d like it to change the date to 30th Sept for Phase 1 (Q3), 31st Dec for Phase 2 (Q4) etc. Automation only seems to allow set to today or push by X days - which doesn’t help in this usecase.

Any help gratefully received!

posts.author: Ashterix (Ash Bladon)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve got as far as creating a formula column to calculate the ‘push’ days from today to the date I want to set the “Date” column to… but it seems there is no way to reference a value in the automation… just put in a fixed number. Really frustrating. Hopefully I’m missing something obvious?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Ashterix

Two possible solutions are 1) a custom Make scenario and 2) General Caster. You can find out more about Make here: What is Make & How can it help you with monday? For more on General Caster, check out the monday marketplace.

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
We Create Custom Solutions
Schedule a 1-on-1 Tutorial Session (for monday, Make or “???”)

posts.author: Ashterix (Ash Bladon)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for these suggestions - really appreciated!

posts.author: victorbeysmith (Victor Bey-Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Same problem here! Crazy that setting a date other than ‘Today’ isn’t possible…

posts.author: ElectricEthen (Ethen Roth)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree! This would be a simple fix that would be very helpful.

posts.author: bterbrueggen (Becky Terbrueggen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree! I would love for there to be a way to set a specific date. We have this problem with yearly contracts…we need the date to stay one date regardless of when we sign.

@monday-team anything like this in the pipeline?

posts.author: JMunoz (Jairo)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also agree that having the option to set a specific date, rather than just the current date, would be incredibly useful and, as mentioned by others, seems like an easy fix. I wish Monday would stop depending on third-party apps for solutions to issues that should be built into the main app.

If this adjustment can’t be made, please consider changing the “set date” label in the “Then do this” section of the automations interface to “set date to today.” to reflect the actual functionality.

posts.author: K.Maiorca (Katherine)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need this! I need to set to a specific date of my choosing, not just ‘today’

posts.author: mightywink (Alicia Reed)
posts.cookedHtml: I also require this in various situations for my boards. Most recently we have to input a date we responded, but in some cases we don’t respond, so a date isn’t required, and we can’t input N/A (or anything text-based) in the date column and for our purposes, we can’t leave it blank. We’ve settled on 1/1/2000 for these situations, and I need an automation to set that date if it was left blank, and I can’t figure out a way to make that happen.
